claudeMd:
# Project Context

You are fixing `server.js` in a Node.js terminal emulator project (mogterm2). The project uses `"type": "module"` in package.json, so all imports must use ESM syntax (`import ... from ...`).

## Bug

The demo server has two problems:
1. `server.listen(port)` binds to `::` (IPv6 only) ÔÇö must bind to `0.0.0.0` for IPv4 access
2. The request handler only returns plain text ÔÇö must serve static files from the project root

## What to Change

Only modify `server.js`. The current file is:
```js
import { createServer } from "node:http";

const port = process.env.PORT || 8080;

const server = createServer((req, res) => {
  res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "text/plain" });
  res.end("Hello from mogterm2!\n");
});

server.listen(port, () => {
  console.log(`Server listening on port ${port}`);
});
```

## Requirements

1. Add ESM imports for `node:fs`, `node:path`, and `node:url` (for `fileURLToPath` to get `__dirname` equivalent)
2. Compute the project root using `path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))`
3. Define a MIME type map: `.html` Ôćĺ `text/html`, `.css` Ôćĺ `text/css`, `.js` Ôćĺ `application/javascript`, `.json` Ôćĺ `application/json`, `.ts` Ôćĺ `text/plain`, default Ôćĺ `application/octet-stream`
4. Replace the request handler to serve static files:
   - Parse `req.url` to get the pathname
   - Map `/` to `/index.html`
   - Reject paths containing `..` with a 403
   - Resolve the path relative to project root
   - Read the file with `fs.readFile`
   - Serve with correct Content-Type; 404 if file not found
5. Change `server.listen(port, ...)` to `server.listen(port, '0.0.0.0', ...)`
6. Update the console.log to show `http://0.0.0.0:${port}`

## Conventions
- ESM imports only (the project has `"type": "module"`)
- Use `node:` protocol prefix for built-in modules (matches existing code)
- Keep it minimal ÔÇö no dependencies, no over-engineering
- Conventional Commits for the commit message: `fix(server): bind to 0.0.0.0 and serve static files`

## Validation
- Start server: `node server.js &`
- Test: `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8080/ | head -5` should return HTML
- Test: `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8080/demo/index.html | head -5` should return HTML
- Test: `curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:8080/nonexistent` should return 404
- Kill the server after testing
- Run existing tests: `npx tsx --test test/**/*.test.ts`

## Gotchas
- Must use `fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)` instead of `__dirname` (ESM has no `__dirname`)
- `fs.readFile` callback-style is fine; no need for promises
- URL pathname may include query strings ÔÇö use `new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost').pathname` to extract just the path
